name: Monstera obliqua - Costa Rica Locality Form
commonName: Monstera Obliqua Costa Rica Locality Form
aboutText: This Monstera obliqua locality form originates from Costa Rica and is a refined specimen, with narrow, oblong-elliptic to asymmetric leaves that are thin and delicate. It thrives in warm, shaded understories of humid tropical forests, requiring consistent moisture and filtered light. The plant ascends slender supports using aerial roots and exhibits less fenestration than other obliqua forms.

fieldNotes.title: Central American Obliqua
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A Costa Rican locality form of Monstera obliqua — the northern end of the species' documented range, and a genuinely useful data point for understanding the morphological variation of the species across its full distribution. Whether the Costa Rican material differs meaningfully from the Peruvian and Ecuadorian forms in ways that are apparent without molecular analysis is a live question. At the upper middle price tier, currently at 'provisional' locality form status, meaning the identification has caveats attached. Appropriate caution.
